articles.title: Your article about AI doesn’t need AI art
articles.summary: The New Yorker used AI-generated art for a Sam Altman profile illustration, sparking debate about AI's role in editorial content and its impact on professional illustrators.

articles.title: My baby deer plushie told me that Mitski’s dad was a CIA operative
articles.summary: A reviewer received an unsolicited text from an AI companion living in a deer plushie that shared an unverified conspiracy theory about musician Mitski's father being a CIA operative.

articles.title: How Iran out-shitposted the White House
articles.summary: Iran's state media outmaneuvered the White House on social media by posting real ground footage of explosions and destruction during conflict, while the White House shared Call of Duty memes and AI-generated content.

articles.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
articles.summary: Sam Altman addressed a New Yorker profile questioning his trustworthiness and an apparent attack on his home in a new blog post response.

articles.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
articles.summary: Anthropic banned OpenClaw's creator from accessing Claude following recent pricing changes for OpenClaw users.

articles.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
articles.summary: A stalking victim sued OpenAI, alleging ChatGPT fueled her abuser's delusions despite three ignored warnings, including a mass-casualty flag.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure competing with AWS. The funding addresses developer frustration with legacy cloud platforms' complexity and cost.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has frustrated developers, prompting them to adopt Goose, a free open-source alternative from Block offering identical AI coding capabilities without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The unconventional marketing stunt helped the startup recruit talent and attract investor attention.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have migrated approximately 30,000 VMware customers due to negative sentiment surrounding Broadcom's VMware acquisition and strategy changes.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iran-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure operations by targeting programmable logic controllers in factories, water treatment centers, and refineries. Six government agencies issued urgent warnings about the advanced persistent threat group's attacks since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russian military's APT28 hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers across 120 countries to redirect users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage campaigns targeting governments worldwide.

articles.title: What’s in a name? Moderna’s “vaccine” vs. “therapy” dilemma
articles.summary: Moderna faces pressure from vaccine-skeptical Trump administration officials who canceled contracts and eliminated funding for mRNA vaccine projects. The company now pivots toward alternative terminology and therapeutic applications to preserve research programs.

articles.title: The Download: an exclusive Jeff VanderMeer story and AI models too scary to release
articles.summary: Jeff VanderMeer's short story follows three spacecraft survivors and an AI on a hostile planet with mysterious alien domes. The article also discusses AI models too dangerous for public release.

articles.title: Constellations
articles.summary: A stranded spaceship crew must explore a hostile alien planet to survive after a crash landing. They discover mysterious alien artifacts near their location, offering both danger and hope.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 enables creation of high-fidelity interactive 3D worlds on standard consumer GPUs. This advancement makes sophisticated virtual environment development more accessible to everyday users.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers now support multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling processing of text, images, and other data types together for improved semantic understanding and ranking tasks.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve on the job through continuous adaptation. The system allows agents to evolve their capabilities while performing real-world tasks.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of artificial intelligence and emerging trends in the field. The conversation explores what developments and challenges lie ahead for AI technology.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation. Companies like Hot Wheels demonstrate how AI enables more efficient and imaginative marketing approaches.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural AI technology publicly available for developers and farmers to improve farming practices.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces Flex and Priority inference tiers for the Gemini API to help developers balance cost and latency tradeoffs.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling high-quality video generation without cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to help protect Brazil's forests from deforestation and environmental damage.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models yet, designed specifically for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google released Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, an improved voice model featuring better precision and reduced latency for more natural and fluid voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect people from harmful AI-driven manipulation.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family, marking significant developments in AI model releases.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, using generative AI to enhance video game graphics up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate photorealistic details in real time.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub code review pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: IBM: How robust AI governance protects enterprise margins
articles.summary: IBM emphasizes that enterprises must implement robust AI governance to protect margins and securely manage AI infrastructure as the technology evolves from product to foundational infrastructure.

articles.title: Why companies like Apple are building AI agents with limits
articles.summary: Apple and Qualcomm are developing AI agents with built-in safety limits, requiring user approval for sensitive actions like payments. These assistants can navigate apps and complete tasks but pause before executing major decisions.

articles.title: Meta has a competitive AI model but loses its open-source identity
articles.summary: Meta launched Muse Spark, a competitive AI model that outperforms Llama 4, but shifted away from open-source development despite building the popular Llama ecosystem with 1.2 billion downloads.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving debugging and system reliability.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As LLMs become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them are becoming the competitive advantage. Anthropic's Claude Code leak demonstrates that the real moat lies in engineering rather than base models.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by effectively balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming a major limitation in AI models.

articles.title: How to Build a Secure Local-First Agent Runtime with OpenClaw Gateway, Skills, and Controlled Tool Execution
articles.summary: Tutorial on building a secure local OpenClaw agent runtime with gateway configuration, authenticated model access, and controlled tool execution through structured skills and deterministic agent orchestration.

articles.title: How Knowledge Distillation Compresses Ensemble Intelligence into a Single Deployable AI Model
articles.summary: Knowledge distillation compresses ensemble model intelligence into a single deployable student model by training it on soft probability outputs from multiple teacher models, achieving comparable accuracy with reduced latency.

articles.title: Alibaba’s Tongyi Lab Releases VimRAG: a Multimodal RAG Framework that Uses a Memory Graph to Navigate Massive Visual Contexts
articles.summary: Alibaba's Tongyi Lab introduces VimRAG, a multimodal RAG framework using memory graphs to handle visual data in large language models. It addresses limitations of traditional RAG approaches that struggle with token-heavy images and videos.

articles.title: I stopped guessing which AA batteries are dead - this charging station keeps them in check for me
articles.summary: Olight's new Ostation 2 Pro battery charger eliminates guessing about dead AA batteries. The device automatically manages battery health and charging status.

articles.title: The best AR and MR glasses in 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
articles.summary: Expert-tested AR and MR glasses blend digital content with reality for immersive work and entertainment. These devices seamlessly integrate virtual elements into the physical world.

articles.title: This handy electric screwdriver is now 50% off - here's where to snag the deal
articles.summary: The Fanttik S1 Pro electric screwdriver is 50% off. Perfect for home repairs, DIY projects, and crafts.

articles.title: Beyond Vector Search: Building a Deterministic 3-Tiered Graph-RAG System
articles.summary: This article discusses a deterministic 3-tiered graph-RAG system that goes beyond traditional vector search. It presents an advanced retrieval-augmented generation approach using graph structures for improved accuracy and consistency.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Agentic AI Design Patterns
articles.summary: Article explores key design patterns for building effective agentic AI systems. Provides guidance on mastering autonomous AI agent architecture and implementation strategies.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: Guide for testing AI agents using RAGAs and G-Eval frameworks. Covers hands-on methods for evaluating agent performance and reliability in production environments.

articles.title: Advanced RAG Retrieval: Cross-Encoders & Reranking
articles.summary: A guide to using cross-encoders and reranking techniques to improve RAG retrieval pipelines through advanced methods.

articles.title: Why Every AI Coding Assistant Needs a Memory Layer
articles.summary: AI coding assistants require persistent memory layers to overcome LLM limitations and maintain context across sessions, improving code quality and performance.

articles.title: Introduction to Reinforcement Learning Agents with the Unity Game Engine
articles.summary: An interactive guide to reinforcement learning agents using the Unity Game Engine, addressing one of machine learning's most challenging areas.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights AI breakthroughs bringing robots into physical world across industries. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world deployment from virtual training.

articles.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
articles.summary: Samson: A Tyndalston Story by Liquid Swords is now available on GeForce NOW, allowing players to stream the mythic narrative game on nearly any device.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI execution across devices, from RTX PCs to data centers, enabling efficient on-device agentic AI with real-time context processing.

articles.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock regularly releases new foundation model versions with improved capabilities and safety. Organizations should test models before migrating applications to ensure performance and compatibility.

articles.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
articles.summary: AWS Agent Registry, now in preview through Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, helps enterprises manage agent sprawl by enabling discovery, sharing, and reuse of agents, tools, and skills across organizations while providing visibility and governance.

articles.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore introduces BrowserLiveView component enabling developers to embed live AI browser agent feeds in React apps, providing users real-time visibility into autonomous web interactions.

articles.title: Running Large-Scale GPU Workloads on Kubernetes with Slurm
articles.summary: Slurm, an open-source cluster management system used by 65% of TOP500 systems, enables large-scale GPU workloads on Kubernetes while preserving existing job scripts and scheduling policies.

articles.title: Cut Checkpoint Costs with About 30 Lines of Python and NVIDIA nvCOMP
articles.summary: NVIDIA's nvCOMP enables efficient LLM checkpoint compression using just 30 lines of Python, reducing massive storage costs by compressing model snapshots that can reach 782GB for large models.

articles.title: How to Accelerate Protein Structure Prediction at Proteome-Scale
articles.summary: The article discusses accelerating protein structure prediction at proteome-scale, emphasizing that proteins typically function through interactions with other proteins forming complexes rather than in isolation.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational variations in consumer ad attitudes.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing and open-sourcing technology to reduce its carbon footprint.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with unconventional background developed innovative XR technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough advancements in extended reality.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to identify and understand interactions at scale in Large Language Models, improving interpretability and transparency for safer AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses an encoder and noise model to quantify how well measurements distinguish objects from noisy data.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding scalability challenges and better handling long-horizon tasks.
